title: The Garden Seat
content:
Its former green is blue and thin,
    And its once firm legs sink in and in;
    Soon it will break down unaware,
    Soon it will break down unaware.

    At night when reddest flowers are black
    Those who once sat thereon come back;
    Quite a row of them sitting there,
    Quite a row of them sitting there.

    With them the seat does not break down,
    Nor winter freeze them, nor floods drown,
    For they are as light as upper air,
    They are as light as upper air!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Hardy delivers a powerful performance in "The Garden Seat"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
